rulings.Case Title: LOUIS TROST VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 17, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY FREER et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN JOAQUIN COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Charles Freer As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 17, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to appoint Charles Freer as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
